name: AP Computer Science A
description: A college-level introduction to computer science using the Java programming language, following the AP Computer Science A curriculum (College Board Course and Exam Description). Covers primitive types, classes and objects, control flow, iteration, arrays and ArrayLists, 2D arrays, inheritance, polymorphism, recursion, and algorithm design. Aligned to the AP exam administered each May. Students leaving this course are prepared for first-semester college CS coursework or to sit the AP exam for credit.

modules.title: Primitive Types & Variables
modules.objectives: Declare and initialize variables of type int, double, boolean, and char; perform arithmetic with operator precedence; understand integer division vs. floating-point division; cast between numeric types; format console output with System.out.println.
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — Java Tutorial for Beginners (variables and operators)
modules.assignment: Write a Java program that converts a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit and from Fahrenheit to Celsius. The program should accept a number and a unit ('C' or 'F') from the user and print the conversion. Demonstrate that you understand integer vs. double division by testing 5/2 and 5.0/2 and explaining the difference in a code comment.

modules.title: Using Objects: String, Math, and Wrapper Classes
modules.titleZh: 使用对象：String、Math 与包装类
modules.objectives: Distinguish primitive types from reference types; create String objects and call methods (length, substring, indexOf, equals, compareTo); use Math methods (sqrt, pow, abs, random); understand Integer and Double wrapper classes; recognize that Strings are immutable.
modules.assignment: Write a program that takes a person's full name as a single String ('First Last' format) and prints: their initials, the length of the last name only, the name in uppercase, and whether the first name comes before the last name alphabetically. Use only String methods — no array splitting yet.

modules.title: Booleans & if Statements
modules.titleZh: 布尔值与 if 语句
modules.objectives: Write boolean expressions using comparison and logical operators (&&, ||, !); apply short-circuit evaluation; structure code with if, if-else, and else-if chains; understand the equals method vs. == for Strings.
modules.readingNote: Runestone CSAwesome — Topic 3: Boolean Expressions and if Statements
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — if-else and Logical Operators
modules.assignment: Write a program that classifies a triangle given three side lengths. Output 'equilateral', 'isosceles', 'scalene', or 'not a triangle' based on the rules (each side less than the sum of the other two). Include three test cases that exercise each branch and one that tests the 'not a triangle' case.

modules.title: Iteration: while and for Loops
modules.titleZh: 迭代：while 与 for 循环
modules.objectives: Write while loops with correct termination conditions; convert between while and for loops; write loops that count up, count down, and step by k; identify and avoid infinite loops; trace loop variables by hand.
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — for and while loops in Java
modules.assignment: Write three loop programs: (1) print the multiplication table from 1 to 10, (2) compute the sum of all even numbers from 2 to N where N is given, (3) print the digits of an integer in reverse order using a while loop with division and modulus. Include trace tables for the first two iterations of each.

modules.title: Nested Loops & Loop Patterns
modules.objectives: Write nested loops to traverse two-dimensional patterns; recognize the standard patterns: counter, accumulator, max/min, sentinel-controlled; understand loop complexity (single loop vs. nested) at an intuitive level.
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — Nested Loops in Java
modules.assignment: Write three nested-loop programs: (1) print a right-angled triangle of asterisks of height N, (2) print a multiplication table grid 1..10 × 1..10 with neat column alignment, (3) given a 2D pattern of letters as a String[][], find the most common letter. Add a comment estimating how many comparisons each program does for input N or grid size G.

modules.title: Writing Classes & Methods
modules.objectives: Define a class with instance variables and methods; pass parameters and return values correctly; understand method signatures and overloading; document methods with concise comments and parameter descriptions.
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — Classes and Methods in Java
modules.video2Note: MIT OpenCourseWare 6.0001 — Designing Programs with Functions
modules.assignment: Design a Rectangle class with width and height instance variables, a constructor, and methods getArea(), getPerimeter(), isSquare(), and scale(double factor). Write a separate driver class that creates three Rectangles and prints each one's area, perimeter, and whether it is a square.

modules.title: Constructors, Encapsulation & this
modules.titleZh: 构造函数、封装与 this 关键字
modules.objectives: Write constructors that initialize instance variables; understand the role of 'this' in disambiguating parameters from fields; apply encapsulation by making instance variables private and exposing only getters/setters; explain why encapsulation matters for invariants.
modules.assignment: Refactor your Rectangle class from Module 6: make all instance variables private, add a no-arg constructor (default 1×1) and a two-arg constructor, validate inputs (no negative dimensions — throw IllegalArgumentException), and add a setWidth/setHeight pair that re-validates. Write tests that demonstrate the validation works.

modules.title: Static vs. Instance
modules.objectives: Distinguish static (class-level) from instance (object-level) members; know when to use static (utility methods, counters, constants) vs. instance; access static members via ClassName.member; understand why main is static.
modules.readingNote: Runestone CSAwesome — Static Variables and Methods
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — Static Keyword in Java
modules.assignment: Add a static class variable 'rectanglesCreated' to your Rectangle class that tracks how many Rectangle objects have been instantiated. Add a static method getRectangleCount(). Demonstrate from a driver: create 5 Rectangles, then print getRectangleCount() — it should be 5. Explain in a comment why this variable cannot be an instance variable.

modules.title: Arrays (1D)
modules.objectives: Declare and initialize 1D arrays; access and modify elements via index; iterate with for and enhanced for-each loops; recognize ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException; pass arrays to methods (reference semantics).
modules.assignment: Write four methods that operate on an int[] array: (1) sum, (2) max, (3) reverse (in place), (4) countAbove(int threshold). Write a driver that creates an array, prints it, calls each method, and prints results. Explain in a comment why reverse modifies the original array but sum returns a new value.

modules.title: ArrayList
modules.objectives: Use the ArrayList class from java.util; add, remove, get, and set elements; iterate with for and for-each loops; understand the difference from primitive arrays (resizable, only holds objects, generic type parameter); convert between ArrayList and array.
modules.assignment: Write a TodoList class backed by an ArrayList<String>. Methods: add(String task), removeAt(int index), markDone(int index) (which prepends '[X] '), printAll(). Write a driver that adds 5 tasks, marks 2 done, removes 1, and prints the list. Then convert the final list to a String[] and print its length.

modules.title: 2D Arrays
modules.objectives: Declare and initialize 2D arrays with [rows][cols]; iterate with nested loops in row-major order; understand 2D arrays as arrays of arrays (jagged arrays possible); apply to grid problems (game boards, image pixels, matrices).
modules.assignment: Implement a Tic-Tac-Toe board as char[3][3]. Write methods: printBoard(), placeMove(int row, int col, char player), isWinner(char player), and isFull(). Demonstrate with a driver that simulates a complete game (alternating X and O) until someone wins or the board is full.

modules.title: Inheritance & Polymorphism
modules.objectives: Define subclasses with extends; call super() in constructors; override methods (with @Override); use polymorphism (a parent reference holding a child object); understand the relationship between Object class and all other classes; cast safely with instanceof.
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — Inheritance and super in Java
modules.assignment: Build a small class hierarchy: Animal (parent) → Dog and Cat (children). Animal has name, age, and a speak() method that prints 'Some sound'. Dog overrides speak() to print 'Woof'; Cat to print 'Meow'. Write a driver that creates an Animal[] containing a Dog and a Cat, and loops through calling speak() — demonstrate polymorphism in action.

modules.title: Recursion
modules.objectives: Identify a base case and a recursive case; trace recursive calls with a call stack diagram; convert simple iterative algorithms to recursive ones; recognize when recursion is natural (tree traversal, divide-and-conquer) vs. forced.
modules.assignment: Implement these methods recursively: factorial(int n), fibonacci(int n), sumDigits(int n), and palindromeCheck(String s). For at least one method, draw a call stack diagram showing every recursive call for an input of 4. Compare runtime intuitively (which one would be slow on n=40 and why).

modules.title: Sorting & Searching Algorithms
modules.objectives: Implement and trace selection sort, insertion sort, and merge sort; implement linear search and binary search; understand the requirement that binary search needs sorted input; recognize O(n²) vs. O(n log n) vs. O(log n) at an intuitive level.
modules.readingNote: Runestone CSAwesome — Topic 11: Sorting and Searching
modules.videoNote: Bro Code — Selection, Insertion, and Merge Sort
modules.practiceNote: Practice-It — BJP4 Chapter 13 Searching and Sorting
modules.assignment: Implement selectionSort, insertionSort, and binarySearch on int[] arrays. Write a driver that builds an array of 20 random ints, sorts it with each algorithm (verify all three give the same sorted result), then runs binarySearch on the sorted output for 5 different target values (3 in the array, 2 not in the array). Print the index returned (or -1).
